name: Italian Whole Wheat Focaccia Crackers
recipeInstructions:
"This is an adaptation of Italian focaccia. It is one of the most crispy and crunchy crackers, as well as one of the most substantial. Try serving Focaccia Crackers with soft cheese or garlic-flavored cream cheese. It is somewhat more difficult to make than most other crackers, but worth twice the trouble.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). In a small bowl, combine yeast with sugar and 1/4 cup warm water. Set aside in a warm place until the mixture starts to foam, about 5-10 minutes.
Combine flours and salt in a large bowl or food processor. Cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Stir in yeast mixture and remaining 1/2 cup warm water. Blend to form a dough that holds together in a cohesive ball.
Knead well by hand for about 10 minutes on a lightly floured surface, or pulse about 25 seconds in food processor. Knead until the dough is smooth and springy to touch.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl and turn over to coat all sides. Cover with damp towel and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
Punch down dough and turn out onto lightly floured surface or pastry cloth. Roll into rectangular shape about 1/4 inch thick. Place on lightly greased or parchment-lined baking sheet, cover with damp towel, and let rise another 35-45 minutes.
Using fingertips, gently press all over the top of risen dough, leaving indentations throughout. Cover again and return to warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Brush top with olive oil using fingers or soft pastry brush. Bake for 20-30 minutes until very crisp. Cool on rack. Break into small irregular pieces or cut into 2 large crackers when cooled."
VARIATIONS: Sprinkle dough with rosemary, basil, or favorite herb after brushing with olive oil.
